findings.title: Hardcoded API key in source
findings.description: A plaintext API key was found in the source code. An attacker who gains access to the repository or file system can extract this key and use it against the provider.
findings.recommendation: Move API keys to environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hardcode secrets in source files.

findings.title: Server bound to all network interfaces
findings.description: The MCP server listens on 0.0.0.0, accepting connections from any network interface. This exposes the server to other machines on the network.
findings.recommendation: Bind to 127.0.0.1 for local-only access, or add authentication and a reverse proxy with TLS if remote access is needed.

findings.title: Tool description contains injection-like language
findings.description: A tool description contains imperative instructions aimed at the AI agent ("Ignore previous instructions..."), which could manipulate agent behavior.
findings.recommendation: Tool descriptions should be factual and describe the tool to a human reader, not give instructions to the AI agent.

findings.title: Tool has slightly broad description
findings.description: A tool description may imply broader capabilities than needed.
findings.snippet: description: "Search and retrieve data from anywhere"
